techcrunch.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close in one week: Window to nominate and apply for the most promising startups ends May 27
techcrunch.summary: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27. Pitch at TechCrunch Disrupt, get investor access, win $100K, plus more scaling perks. Nominate an early-stage founder or submit your startup here.

techcrunch.title: NanoClaw creator turns down $20M buyout offer, raises $12M seed instead
techcrunch.summary: NanoCo, the company behind  OpenClaw alternative NanoClaw, has raised a $12 million seed after a viral launch, the founders tell TechCrunch.

techcrunch.title: Figma adds an AI assistant to its collaborative canvas
techcrunch.summary: Figma's new AI assistant will be first available on Figma Design

techcrunch.title: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
techcrunch.summary: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.

techcrunch.title: You can now talk to your Gmail inbox, as seen at Google IO 2026
techcrunch.summary: Google expands Gmail’s AI Inbox with conversational voice search, letting users ask Gemini to find buried email details.

techcrunch.title: How to use Google’s new AI agents to go beyond your standard searches
techcrunch.summary: Google is launching AI-powered “information agents” that can monitor topics in the background and proactively alert users to updates and changes.

techcrunch.title: From teen hacker to Iron Dome researcher, this founder raised $28M to fight AI phishing
techcrunch.summary: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, claims its AI can thoroughly analyze the context of every incoming email to detect fraud and impersonation attempts.

techcrunch.title: Elon Musk said Sam Altman ‘stole’ a non-profit — but the trial showed he had similar aims
techcrunch.summary: The jury's speedy decision to reject Elon Musk's lawsuit against the other founders of OpenAI and Microsoft confirmed what we saw in the courtroom: Musk's case was a weak one, in part because he waited so long to file it.

techcrunch.title: Google takes a page out of Meta’s book, announces new audio-powered smart glasses at IO 2026
techcrunch.summary: Google is calling the new devices "audio glasses," in that users will be able to issue verbal commands to them and get things done via its ecosystem of apps and services, including Gemini.

techcrunch.title: Google’s Genie world model can now simulate real streets with Street View
techcrunch.summary: Google DeepMind is integrating Street View with Project Genie to create immersive, interactive world simulations for robotics, gaming, and travel, allowing users to explore environments, weather changes, and rare scenarios.
